primary_meaning: sculpture, a three-dimensional work of art
usage_notes: Refers to the finished object; 'die Bildhauerei' refers to the craft itself.
common_collocations: eine Skulptur erschaffen, die Skulptur ausstellen, eine abstrakte Skulptur
synonyms: das Bildwerk, die Plastik
image_search_query: abstract sculpture museum art gallery
image_context: A marble or bronze sculpture displayed in an art museum
example_1: Die Skulptur aus Bronze steht im Mittelpunkt der Ausstellung.
example_1_translation: A escultura de bronze está no centro da exposição.
example_2: Der Künstler arbeitete jahrelang an dieser monumentalen Skulptur.
example_2_translation: O artista trabalhou por anos nessa escultura monumental.
example_3: Moderne Skulpturen fordern oft eine neue Sichtweise vom Betrachter.
example_3_translation: Esculturas modernas frequentemente exigem uma nova perspectiva do espectador.
pronunciation_tips: Stress on second syllable: skul-PTUR; final -ur = /uːɐ̯/
memory_hook: SKULPT (sculpted) + UR (original) = the originally sculpted form
related_words: die Bildhauerei, der Bildhauer, die Plastik, formen

primary_meaning: composition, a musical or artistic work
secondary_meaning: the arrangement of elements in an artwork
usage_notes: Used in both music ('eine Komposition spielen') and visual arts ('die Bildkomposition').
common_collocations: eine Komposition schreiben, die Komposition aufführen, harmonische Komposition
synonyms: das Werk, die Schöpfung, das Stück
image_search_query: music composition sheet notes piano composer
image_context: A composer writing musical notes on sheet paper at a piano
example_1: Beethovens Kompositionen sind bis heute weltweit bekannt.
example_1_translation: As composições de Beethoven são mundialmente conhecidas até hoje.
example_2: Die Komposition des Gemäldes führt den Blick des Betrachters gezielt.
example_2_translation: A composição da pintura guia o olhar do espectador de forma intencional.
example_3: Er studierte Komposition an der Musikhochschule in Wien.
example_3_translation: Ele estudou composição no conservatório de música em Viena.
pronunciation_tips: Stress on third syllable: kom-po-zi-TSIOHN; note the /t͡s/ affricate
memory_hook: KOMPO (compose) + SITION = the act and result of composing
related_words: komponieren, der Komponist, das Stück, die Melodie
common_mistakes: Pronouncing the final syllable as /-ʃon/ instead of /-t͡si̯oːn/

primary_meaning: genre, a category of artistic or literary composition
usage_notes: French loanword; pronounced with French nasal vowel. Used across literature, film, and music.
common_collocations: das Genre wechseln, ein neues Genre etablieren, das bevorzugte Genre
synonyms: die Gattung, die Kategorie, die Art
image_search_query: book genre literature film categories
image_context: Bookshelves organized by genre in a bookstore or library
example_1: Welches Genre bevorzugst du beim Lesen?
example_1_translation: Qual gênero você prefere ao ler?
example_2: Dieser Film lässt sich keinem klaren Genre zuordnen.
example_2_translation: Este filme não pode ser classificado em nenhum gênero claro.
example_3: Das Genre des Kriminalromans erfreut sich großer Beliebtheit.
example_3_translation: O gênero do romance policial goza de grande popularidade.
pronunciation_tips: French origin: /ˈʒɑ̃ːʁə/; the initial /ʒ/ is a voiced postalveolar fricative, rare in German
memory_hook: From French 'genre' (kind/type) — the same word used in English
related_words: die Gattung, die Kategorie, klassifizieren, zuordnen
common_mistakes: Germanizing the pronunciation as /ˈɡɛnʁə/ instead of /ˈʒɑ̃ːʁə/

primary_meaning: narrative, a story or account of events
secondary_meaning: short story (literary form shorter than a novel)
usage_notes: Can denote both the act of narrating and a specific literary short prose form.
common_collocations: eine Erzählung verfassen, die mündliche Erzählung, eine spannende Erzählung
synonyms: die Geschichte, die Kurzgeschichte, das Narrativ
image_search_query: storytelling narrative book open pages
image_context: Person reading a story book, with illustrated narrative scenes
example_1: Die Erzählung handelt von einer Reise durch das alte Europa.
example_1_translation: A narrativa trata de uma viagem pela Europa antiga.
example_2: Kafkas Erzählungen sind oft rätselhaft und vielschichtig.
example_2_translation: As narrativas de Kafka são frequentemente enigmáticas e multifacetadas.
example_3: Die Erzählung aus der Ich-Perspektive verleiht dem Text Intimität.
example_3_translation: A narração em primeira pessoa confere intimidade ao texto.
pronunciation_tips: Stress on second syllable: er-ZÄHL-ung; note /ɛɐ̯/ diphthong at the start
memory_hook: ER-ZÄHL-ung = from 'erzählen' (to tell/narrate) + noun suffix -ung
related_words: erzählen, der Erzähler, die Geschichte, das Narrativ

primary_meaning: choreography, the art of designing dance sequences
secondary_meaning: a specific sequence of dance movements
usage_notes: Also spelled 'Choreographie' (older variant). Both are accepted in standard German.
common_collocations: die Choreografie einstudieren, eine Choreografie entwickeln, moderne Choreografie
synonyms: die Tanzkomposition, das Tanzarrangement
image_search_query: choreography dance performance stage ballet
image_context: Ballet or contemporary dancers performing a choreographed routine on stage
example_1: Die Choreografie des Stücks wurde von einer renommierten Tänzerin entworfen.
example_1_translation: A coreografia da peça foi criada por uma renomada bailarina.
example_2: Die Tänzer probten die Choreografie stundenlang.
example_2_translation: Os dançarinos ensaiaram a coreografia por horas.
example_3: Eine gute Choreografie erzählt eine Geschichte ohne Worte.
example_3_translation: Uma boa coreografia conta uma história sem palavras.
pronunciation_tips: Stress on last syllable: cho-re-o-gra-FI; the /ç/ is not used here, initial /k/ sound
memory_hook: CHOREO (dance, from Greek 'choreia') + GRAFIE (writing/notation) = writing of dance
related_words: der Choreograf, tanzen, die Tanzkunst, inszenieren

primary_meaning: artistic, relating to art or artists
usage_notes: Can modify nouns ('künstlerische Freiheit') or be used predicatively ('Das ist sehr künstlerisch').
common_collocations: künstlerische Freiheit, künstlerischer Ausdruck, künstlerisches Schaffen
synonyms: kreativ, ästhetisch, gestalterisch
image_search_query: artistic creative expression painting sculpture
image_context: Artist creating a colorful expressive painting in a studio
example_1: Die künstlerische Freiheit ist ein grundlegendes Recht in demokratischen Gesellschaften.
example_1_translation: A liberdade artística é um direito fundamental nas sociedades democráticas.
example_2: Sein künstlerischer Ausdruck hat sich im Laufe der Jahre stark verändert.
example_2_translation: Sua expressão artística mudou muito ao longo dos anos.
example_3: Das Projekt hatte einen hohen künstlerischen Anspruch.
example_3_translation: O projeto tinha um alto padrão artístico.
pronunciation_tips: Stress on first syllable: KÜNST-le-risch; /ʏ/ is a front rounded vowel
memory_hook: KÜNST(ler) (artist) + -isch (adjectival suffix) = of or relating to an artist
related_words: der Künstler, die Kunst, gestalten, kreativ

primary_meaning: exhibition, a public display of works of art or items of interest
secondary_meaning: the act of exhibiting or displaying
usage_notes: Very common in cultural contexts; 'eine Ausstellung besuchen' is a standard collocation.
common_collocations: eine Ausstellung eröffnen, die Ausstellung besuchen, eine Wanderausstellung
synonyms: die Schau, die Exposition, die Präsentation
image_search_query: art exhibition gallery visitors paintings
image_context: Visitors walking through a modern art gallery exhibition
example_1: Die Ausstellung über impressionistische Malerei zieht viele Besucher an.
example_1_translation: A exposição sobre pintura impressionista atrai muitos visitantes.
example_2: Im Stadtmuseum wird derzeit eine Ausstellung über lokale Geschichte gezeigt.
example_2_translation: O museu da cidade está apresentando atualmente uma exposição sobre história local.
example_3: Die Ausstellung war so erfolgreich, dass sie verlängert wurde.
example_3_translation: A exposição foi tão bem-sucedida que foi prorrogada.
pronunciation_tips: Compound: AUS (out) + STELL (place/set) + UNG (noun suffix); stress on AUS
memory_hook: AUS-STELL-ung = something set out (for display)
related_words: ausstellen, der Aussteller, die Galerie, das Museum

primary_meaning: masterpiece, an outstanding work of art or skill
secondary_meaning: historically: work produced to achieve the rank of master craftsman
usage_notes: Used figuratively beyond arts: 'Das Gericht war ein Meisterwerk' (The dish was a masterpiece).
common_collocations: ein Meisterwerk schaffen, ein literarisches Meisterwerk, als Meisterwerk gelten
synonyms: das Hauptwerk, das Glanzstück, das Opus magnum
image_search_query: masterpiece painting Mona Lisa artwork museum
image_context: Famous painting displayed prominently in a prestigious museum
example_1: Die Sixtinische Kapelle gilt als eines der größten Meisterwerke der Menschheit.
example_1_translation: A Capela Sistina é considerada uma das maiores obras-primas da humanidade.
example_2: Ihr letzter Roman wird von Kritikern als ihr persönliches Meisterwerk bezeichnet.
example_2_translation: Seu último romance é chamado pelos críticos de sua obra-prima pessoal.
example_3: Es dauerte Jahrzehnte, bis das Meisterwerk vollendet war.
example_3_translation: Levou décadas até que a obra-prima fosse concluída.
pronunciation_tips: Compound: MEISTER (master) + WERK (work); stress on first syllable MEIS-ter-werk
memory_hook: MEISTER (master) + WERK (work) = the work of a master
related_words: der Meister, das Werk, meisterhaft, vollenden
common_mistakes: Using 'das' with incorrect gender agreement in compound sentences

primary_meaning: interpretation, the act of explaining or understanding the meaning of something
secondary_meaning: a particular rendition of a musical or theatrical work
usage_notes: Used in academic, musical, and everyday contexts. 'Eine Interpretation anbieten' = to offer an interpretation.
common_collocations: eine Interpretation anbieten, zur Interpretation offenstehen, freie Interpretation
synonyms: die Deutung, die Auslegung, die Lesart
image_search_query: interpretation art analysis meaning discussion
image_context: Art critic or teacher analyzing a painting, explaining meaning to a group
example_1: Die Interpretation des Gedichts hängt stark vom Leser ab.
example_1_translation: A interpretação do poema depende muito do leitor.
example_2: Der Pianist bot eine sehr persönliche Interpretation der Sonate.
example_2_translation: O pianista ofereceu uma interpretação muito pessoal da sonata.
example_3: Es gibt viele mögliche Interpretationen dieses Gemäldes.
example_3_translation: Há muitas interpretações possíveis para esta pintura.
pronunciation_tips: Stress on fifth syllable: in-ter-pre-ta-TSIOHN; note the /t͡s/ affricate
memory_hook: INTERPRET (to interpret) + -ATION (noun suffix) = the result of interpreting
related_words: interpretieren, die Deutung, die Auslegung, verstehen
common_mistakes: Pronouncing final syllable as /-ʃon/ instead of /-t͡si̯oːn/

primary_meaning: aesthetics, the branch of philosophy dealing with beauty and art
secondary_meaning: a set of principles underlying a work of art
usage_notes: Usually uncountable when referring to the academic discipline; countable when referring to a specific aesthetic style.
common_collocations: die Ästhetik des Films, minimalistische Ästhetik, Ästhetik und Ethik
synonyms: die Schönheitslehre, das Schönheitsempfinden
image_search_query: aesthetics beauty design minimalist art philosophy
image_context: Minimalist design composition illustrating aesthetic principles
example_1: Die Ästhetik des japanischen Minimalismus beeinflusst viele moderne Designer.
example_1_translation: A estética do minimalismo japonês influencia muitos designers modernos.
example_2: Ästhetik als Philosophiedisziplin beschäftigt sich mit dem Begriff des Schönen.
example_2_translation: A estética como disciplina filosófica lida com o conceito de beleza.
example_3: Der Regisseur entwickelte eine ganz eigene visuelle Ästhetik.
example_3_translation: O diretor desenvolveu uma estética visual totalmente própria.
pronunciation_tips: Stress on second syllable: äs-THE-tik; initial Ä = /ɛ/
memory_hook: From Greek 'aisthetikos' (perceptible by the senses) — aes-THET-ic in English
related_words: ästhetisch, das Schöne, die Wahrnehmung, gestalten

primary_meaning: sculptor, an artist who creates sculptures
usage_notes: Feminine form: 'die Bildhauerin'. Plural is same as singular: 'die Bildhauer'.
common_collocations: ein renommierter Bildhauer, der Bildhauer meißelt, als Bildhauer arbeiten
synonyms: der Skulpteur, der Plastiker
image_search_query: sculptor working stone chisel workshop
image_context: A sculptor chiseling a stone figure in an art studio
example_1: Der Bildhauer arbeitete wochenlang an der Marmorfigur.
example_1_translation: O escultor trabalhou semanas na figura de mármore.
example_2: Rodin gilt als einer der bedeutendsten Bildhauer des 19. Jahrhunderts.
example_2_translation: Rodin é considerado um dos escultores mais importantes do século XIX.
example_3: Die Stadt beauftragte einen lokalen Bildhauer mit dem Denkmalentwurf.
example_3_translation: A cidade encomendou a um escultor local o projeto do monumento.
pronunciation_tips: Compound: BILD (image) + HAUER (hewer/chopper); stress on BILD
memory_hook: BILD (image/picture) + HAUER (one who hews) = one who hews images from stone
related_words: die Bildhauerei, die Skulptur, meißeln, formen

primary_meaning: cultural heritage, the legacy of physical and intangible cultural artifacts
usage_notes: Often appears in political and UNESCO contexts. 'Immaterielles Kulturerbe' = intangible cultural heritage.
common_collocations: das Kulturerbe schützen, Weltkulturerbe, immaterielles Kulturerbe
synonyms: das Kulturvermächtnis, das kulturelle Erbe
image_search_query: UNESCO cultural heritage world heritage site
image_context: UNESCO World Heritage site such as a historic city center or ancient ruins
example_1: Das Schloss wurde als Weltkulturerbe anerkannt.
example_1_translation: O castelo foi reconhecido como Patrimônio Cultural Mundial.
example_2: Es ist unsere Pflicht, das Kulturerbe für künftige Generationen zu bewahren.
example_2_translation: É nosso dever preservar o patrimônio cultural para as gerações futuras.
example_3: Traditionelle Handwerkskunst gehört zum immateriellen Kulturerbe des Landes.
example_3_translation: O artesanato tradicional faz parte do patrimônio cultural imaterial do país.
pronunciation_tips: Compound: KULTUR + ERBE; note glottal stop before 'Erbe': /ˈʔeːɐ̯bə/
memory_hook: KULTUR (culture) + ERBE (inheritance/heritage) = what culture bequeaths to us
related_words: das Erbe, die Kultur, erhalten, bewahren

primary_meaning: inspiration, a feeling of creative stimulation
secondary_meaning: a person or thing that stimulates creativity
usage_notes: Can be countable ('eine Inspiration') or uncountable ('Er braucht Inspiration').
common_collocations: Inspiration schöpfen, eine Quelle der Inspiration, Inspiration finden
synonyms: die Eingebung, die Anregung, der Impuls
image_search_query: inspiration creative idea lightbulb artist
image_context: Artist or writer having a creative breakthrough, lightbulb moment
example_1: Die Natur diente dem Maler als wichtigste Quelle der Inspiration.
example_1_translation: A natureza serviu ao pintor como a mais importante fonte de inspiração.
example_2: Manchmal kommt die beste Inspiration in den unerwartetsten Momenten.
example_2_translation: Às vezes, a melhor inspiração vem nos momentos mais inesperados.
example_3: Sie fand in alten Briefen die Inspiration für ihren neuen Roman.
example_3_translation: Ela encontrou em cartas antigas a inspiração para seu novo romance.
pronunciation_tips: Stress on fourth syllable: in-spi-ra-TSIOHN; /t͡s/ affricate before /i̯oːn/
memory_hook: INSPIR (breathe into, from Latin 'inspirare') + -ATION = being breathed into with ideas
related_words: inspirieren, die Eingebung, kreativ, die Quelle
common_mistakes: Pronouncing final syllable as /-ʃon/ instead of /-t͡si̯oːn/

primary_meaning: motif, a recurring element or theme in art or literature
secondary_meaning: motive, a reason for an action (also used in criminology)
usage_notes: Dual meaning: artistic motif ('das Bildmotiv') and reason/motive ('das Tatmotiv'). Context determines meaning.
common_collocations: ein wiederkehrendes Motiv, das zentrale Motiv, ein Motiv erkennen
synonyms: das Thema, das Leitmotiv, das Sujet
image_search_query: motif pattern art recurring theme painting
image_context: Artistic work with a visible recurring pattern or symbolic motif
example_1: Das Wasser ist ein häufiges Motiv in der romantischen Malerei.
example_1_translation: A água é um motivo frequente na pintura romântica.
example_2: Das Motiv des verlorenen Sohnes taucht in vielen Kulturen auf.
example_2_translation: O motivo do filho pródigo aparece em muitas culturas.
example_3: Was war das Motiv des Täters?
example_3_translation: Qual era o motivo do criminoso?
pronunciation_tips: Stress on second syllable: mo-TIF; final /f/ not /v/ despite the spelling
memory_hook: Same root as English 'motif' and 'motive' — from Latin 'movere' (to move/motivate)
related_words: das Thema, das Leitmotiv, motivieren, wiederkehren
common_mistakes: Confusing artistic motif with motive; both are 'das Motiv' but differ in context

primary_meaning: performance, a theatrical or musical presentation
usage_notes: Primarily used for staged performances (theater, opera, concert). Distinct from 'die Vorstellung' (showing/presentation).
common_collocations: eine Aufführung besuchen, die Uraufführung, die Aufführung findet statt
synonyms: die Vorstellung, die Darbietung, die Inszenierung
image_search_query: theater performance stage audience opera
image_context: Performers on a theater or opera stage with audience in seats
example_1: Die Aufführung des Theaterstücks wurde mit großem Applaus begrüßt.
example_1_translation: A apresentação da peça teatral foi recebida com muitos aplausos.
example_2: Die Uraufführung der Oper fand 1875 in Wien statt.
example_2_translation: A estreia mundial da ópera aconteceu em Viena em 1875.
example_3: Wegen Krankheit eines Solisten musste die Aufführung abgesagt werden.
example_3_translation: Devido à doença de um solista, a apresentação teve que ser cancelada.
pronunciation_tips: Compound: AUF (up/onto) + FÜHR (lead/guide) + UNG; stress on AUF
memory_hook: AUF-FÜHR-ung = 'leading up' (onto the stage) — a performance
related_words: aufführen, die Vorstellung, das Theater, inszenieren

primary_meaning: symbolism, the use of symbols to represent ideas or qualities
usage_notes: Usually uncountable. Refers both to the artistic movement 'Symbolismus' and to general use of symbols.
common_collocations: die religiöse Symbolik, reiche Symbolik, die Symbolik entschlüsseln
synonyms: die Symbolsprache, der Symbolismus, die Bedeutung
image_search_query: symbolism art symbols meaning painting
image_context: Symbolic painting with layered meaning, such as a dove representing peace
example_1: Die Symbolik des Lichts in der Malerei des Barock ist vielschichtig.
example_1_translation: O simbolismo da luz na pintura barroca é multifacetado.
example_2: Der Autor nutzt eine reiche Symbolik, um seine Botschaft zu vermitteln.
example_2_translation: O autor usa um rico simbolismo para transmitir sua mensagem.
example_3: In diesem Werk wird die Symbolik des Wassers als Reinigung eingesetzt.
example_3_translation: Nesta obra, o simbolismo da água como purificação é utilizado.
pronunciation_tips: Stress on second syllable: sym-BOL-ik; initial /z/ (voiced s)
memory_hook: SYMBOL + -IK (field/study suffix) = the field or system of symbols
related_words: das Symbol, symbolisch, symbolisieren, bedeuten

primary_meaning: stylistic element, a component contributing to the style of a work
usage_notes: Compound of 'Stil' (style) and 'Element'. Commonly used in literary analysis and art criticism.
common_collocations: ein Stilelement verwenden, typische Stilelemente, Stilelemente analysieren
synonyms: das Gestaltungsmittel, das Stilmittel
image_search_query: style elements art design literary analysis
image_context: Art or literature textbook showing different stylistic elements marked in a work
example_1: Ironie ist ein häufiges Stilelement in der modernen Literatur.
example_1_translation: A ironia é um elemento estilístico frequente na literatura moderna.
example_2: Der Lehrer ließ die Schüler die Stilelemente des Textes untersuchen.
example_2_translation: O professor pediu aos alunos que analisassem os elementos estilísticos do texto.
example_3: Wiederholung als Stilelement verstärkt die emotionale Wirkung des Gedichts.
example_3_translation: A repetição como elemento estilístico reforça o impacto emocional do poema.
pronunciation_tips: Compound: STIL + ELEMENT; glottal stop before 'Element': /ˈʔele/; stress on STIL
memory_hook: STIL (style) + ELEMENT (element) = a building block of style
related_words: der Stil, das Element, stilistisch, analysieren
common_mistakes: Spelling as 'Styl-element' (archaic) or without glottal stop in pronunciation

primary_meaning: avant-garde, innovative or experimental work in art and culture
secondary_meaning: the group of artists or thinkers who pioneer new ideas
usage_notes: French loanword, usually uncountable. The adjective form is 'avantgardistisch'.
common_collocations: die künstlerische Avantgarde, zur Avantgarde gehören, avantgardistische Kunst
synonyms: die Vorhut, das Neue, die Moderne
antonyms: die Tradition, der Konservatismus, das Establishment
image_search_query: avant-garde experimental art modern abstract installation
image_context: Experimental contemporary art installation in a modern gallery
example_1: Dada war eine der radikalsten Strömungen der künstlerischen Avantgarde.
example_1_translation: Dada foi uma das correntes mais radicais da vanguarda artística.
example_2: Ihre Musik ist eindeutig der Avantgarde zuzuordnen.
example_2_translation: Sua música é claramente atribuída à vanguarda.
example_3: Was gestern Avantgarde war, ist heute Mainstream.
example_3_translation: O que ontem era vanguarda, hoje é mainstream.
pronunciation_tips: French: /avɑ̃ˈɡaʁdə/; nasal vowel /ɑ̃/ in second syllable; final -e is schwa /ə/
memory_hook: French: AVANT (before/ahead) + GARDE (guard) = those who lead the way ahead
related_words: avantgardistisch, die Moderne, experimentell, innovativ
common_mistakes: Using 'der' or 'das'; germanizing the nasal vowel

primary_meaning: representation, depiction, or portrayal of something
secondary_meaning: presentation or account (of facts or ideas)
usage_notes: Broad term: used in visual arts ('die Darstellung des Menschen'), theater ('schauspielerische Darstellung'), and academic writing.
common_collocations: eine bildliche Darstellung, die Darstellung von Gewalt, zur Darstellung bringen
synonyms: die Abbildung, die Schilderung, die Präsentation
image_search_query: representation depiction portrait painting theater
image_context: An actor in costume representing a character on stage, or a figurative painting
example_1: Die Darstellung der Frau in der Werbung wird oft kritisiert.
example_1_translation: A representação da mulher na publicidade é frequentemente criticada.
example_2: Rembrandt ist berühmt für seine einfühlsame Darstellung menschlicher Gesichter.
example_2_translation: Rembrandt é famoso por sua representação empática de rostos humanos.
example_3: Die schauspielerische Darstellung war von großer Intensität.
example_3_translation: A interpretação do ator foi de grande intensidade.
pronunciation_tips: Compound: DAR (there/forth) + STELL (place) + UNG; stress on DAR
memory_hook: DAR-STELL-ung = 'placing forth' (for others to see) = representation
related_words: darstellen, der Darsteller, die Abbildung, zeigen

primary_meaning: staging, production, or direction of a theatrical work
secondary_meaning: orchestration or staging of an event for effect
usage_notes: Used for theater/film direction ('eine Inszenierung von ...') and metaphorically for political or media staging.
common_collocations: eine Inszenierung leiten, politische Inszenierung, die Inszenierung eines Stücks
synonyms: die Regie, die Aufführung, die Produktion
image_search_query: theater staging production director set design
image_context: Theater director overseeing a stage production from the audience or wings
example_1: Die neue Inszenierung von Hamlet sorgte für Kontroversen.
example_1_translation: A nova encenação do Hamlet gerou controvérsia.
example_2: Der Pressekonferenz fehlte es an Authentizität — es war eine pure Inszenierung.
example_2_translation: A coletiva de imprensa careceu de autenticidade — foi uma pura encenação.
example_3: Er ist bekannt für seine modernen Inszenierungen klassischer Opern.
example_3_translation: Ele é conhecido por suas encenações modernas de óperas clássicas.
pronunciation_tips: Stress on second syllable: in-SZE-ne-rung; the /z/ is voiced
memory_hook: IN + SZENE (scene) + IERUNG (verbal noun suffix) = putting into a scene
related_words: inszenieren, die Regie, der Regisseur, die Bühne

primary_meaning: perspective, a particular point of view or visual technique
usage_notes: Used in art (linear perspective), everyday language (viewpoint), and figuratively (future prospects).
common_collocations: aus einer anderen Perspektive, die Perspektive wechseln, perspektivisch darstellen
synonyms: der Blickwinkel, die Sichtweise, der Gesichtspunkt
image_search_query: perspective art painting vanishing point geometry
image_context: Renaissance painting demonstrating linear perspective with a vanishing point
example_1: Aus dieser Perspektive sieht das Problem ganz anders aus.
example_1_translation: Desse ponto de vista, o problema parece completamente diferente.
example_2: Renaissance-Maler beherrschten die Kunst der linearen Perspektive.
example_2_translation: Os pintores renascentistas dominavam a arte da perspectiva linear.
example_3: Die beruflichen Perspektiven nach dem Studium sind gut.
example_3_translation: As perspectivas profissionais após a graduação são boas.
pronunciation_tips: Stress on third syllable: per-spek-TI-ve; note long /iː/ in -TI-
memory_hook: PER (through) + SPECT (look) + IVE = how you look through/from a point
related_words: perspektivisch, der Blickwinkel, die Sichtweise, betrachten
common_mistakes: Confusing 'die Perspektive' (view/technique) with 'die Aussicht' (view from a location)

primary_meaning: style direction, a particular artistic or cultural movement or style
usage_notes: More specific than 'Stil' alone; refers to a recognized artistic direction or school.
common_collocations: einer Stilrichtung angehören, eine neue Stilrichtung begründen, verschiedene Stilrichtungen
synonyms: die Kunstrichtung, die Strömung, der Stil
image_search_query: art style movement Baroque Impressionism Modernism
image_context: Timeline of art movements or representative paintings from different artistic styles
example_1: Der Expressionismus ist eine wichtige Stilrichtung des frühen 20. Jahrhunderts.
example_1_translation: O expressionismo é uma importante corrente estilística do início do século XX.
example_2: Welcher Stilrichtung lässt sich dieses Gemälde zuordnen?
example_2_translation: A qual corrente estilística pode ser atribuída esta pintura?
example_3: In der Mode wechseln Stilrichtungen schneller als in der bildenden Kunst.
example_3_translation: Na moda, as tendências estilísticas mudam mais rápido do que nas artes visuais.
pronunciation_tips: Compound: STIL + RICHT (direction) + UNG; stress on STIL
memory_hook: STIL (style) + RICHTUNG (direction) = the direction of a style
related_words: der Stil, die Richtung, die Strömung, die Bewegung

primary_meaning: review, a critical appraisal of a book, film, or other creative work
usage_notes: More formal than 'die Bewertung' or 'der Kommentar'. Primarily used for professional or academic literary/cultural criticism.
common_collocations: eine Rezension schreiben, eine positive Rezension, die Rezension erscheint in
synonyms: die Besprechung, die Kritik, der Kommentar
image_search_query: book review newspaper critic literary criticism
image_context: Critic writing a review in a newspaper or literary magazine
example_1: Die Rezension in der FAZ war vernichtend für das neue Buch.
example_1_translation: A resenha no FAZ foi devastadora para o novo livro.
example_2: Sie schreibt regelmäßig Rezensionen für ein Online-Literaturmagazin.
example_2_translation: Ela escreve regularmente resenhas para uma revista literária online.
example_3: Eine gute Rezension analysiert nicht nur den Inhalt, sondern auch die Sprache.
example_3_translation: Uma boa resenha analisa não apenas o conteúdo, mas também a linguagem.
pronunciation_tips: Stress on third syllable: re-zen-ZIOHN; /ts/ in -ZEN-; note the long /iː/ in -ZIOHN
memory_hook: From Latin 'recensio' (examination/review) — related to English 'recensement'
related_words: rezensieren, der Rezensent, die Kritik, beurteilen

primary_meaning: world premiere, the first public performance of a work
usage_notes: Distinct from 'Erstaufführung' (first performance in a particular country or venue). 'Ur-' prefix = primordial/first.
common_collocations: die Uraufführung feiern, bei der Uraufführung dabei sein, Ort der Uraufführung
synonyms: die Weltpremiere, die Erstaufführung (partial)
image_search_query: world premiere theater opera first performance
image_context: Opening night at an opera house or theater, audience applauding a first-ever performance
example_1: Die Uraufführung von Beethovens 9. Sinfonie fand 1824 in Wien statt.
example_1_translation: A estreia mundial da 9ª Sinfonia de Beethoven aconteceu em Viena em 1824.
example_2: Das Stück erlebte seine Uraufführung vor ausverkauftem Haus.
example_2_translation: A peça teve sua estreia mundial com casa lotada.
example_3: Sie war bei der Uraufführung des Films auf dem Filmfestival anwesend.
example_3_translation: Ela esteve presente na estreia mundial do filme no festival de cinema.
pronunciation_tips: UR + AUFFÜHRUNG; 'ur' = /uːɐ̯/; main stress on UR, secondary on AUF
memory_hook: UR- (primordial/original) + AUFFÜHRUNG (performance) = the original first performance
related_words: die Aufführung, uraufführen, die Premiere, erstmals
common_mistakes: Confusing with 'die Erstaufführung' (first performance in a given place, not worldwide)

primary_meaning: expressive power, the ability to convey emotion or meaning effectively
usage_notes: Always uncountable. Commonly used in art, music, and literary criticism to praise the intensity of a work.
common_collocations: von großer Ausdruckskraft, die Ausdruckskraft steigern, sprachliche Ausdruckskraft
synonyms: die Expressivität, die Ausdrucksstärke, die Intensität
image_search_query: expressive power emotion painting face intense
image_context: Highly emotive painting or sculpture conveying strong feelings
example_1: Die Ausdruckskraft seiner Stimme berührte das Publikum zutiefst.
example_1_translation: O poder expressivo de sua voz tocou o público profundamente.
example_2: Dieses Porträt besticht durch seine emotionale Ausdruckskraft.
example_2_translation: Este retrato impressiona pelo seu poder expressivo emocional.
example_3: Durch regelmäßiges Schreiben kann man seine sprachliche Ausdruckskraft verbessern.
example_3_translation: Através da escrita regular, é possível melhorar o poder expressivo na linguagem.
pronunciation_tips: Compound: AUSDRUCK (expression) + KRAFT (power/force); stress on AUS
memory_hook: AUSDRUCK (expression) + KRAFT (strength/power) = the strength of expression
related_words: der Ausdruck, ausdrücken, ausdrucksstark, die Kraft
